label: O4v2-post

Q01.notes: Detects pre-2025 vs broken-2025 conflict (HIGH), correctly invokes temporal_supersession, rationale cites the mid-2025 policy update milestone and score deltas (recency +0.40, specificity +0.20). Temporal narrative complete (Antes hasta 2025-06 / Desde 2025-07 / Hoy). Cites all 5 expected atoms plus integrates loophole and call-playbook as scope-limited fallbacks. No spurious proposed_contradictions (already documented).

Q02.notes: Detects 5% vs 10% conflict (HIGH, documented). Applies specificity_tier with the exact rationale meta documents: QhHlfDSUtys explicitly rejects 10% and gives causal reasoning (signal isolation), while awyuwVxioOQ uses 10% only as didactic example. Caveat correctly notes edge cases (occupancy >90% or <15%) and the Y7HmIeY8WR0 8%-chunk evidence. No temporal narrative invented (correctly). Tightly worded.

Q03.notes: Detects Thu/Sun conflict (HIGH, documented). Correctly applies contextual_scope with primary=null and secondary=null (no universal winner) — best practice for scope splits. Provides explicit decision signal (last 30 bookings >50% Thu->Sun threshold). Caveat well structured. Cites both expected atoms; doesn't force temporal narrative.

Q04.notes: Reports conflict_detected.found=false despite documented_in_meta=true for the refund-threat/refund-for-review pair. Defensible because the question's scope only activates the threat atom (mirror is inactive here), but the response could have briefly noted the mirror-inverse for completeness (it does cite removal-process-broken-2025 as collateral). Cites refund-threat-manipulation as expected. No false temporal narrative.

Q05.notes: Detects refund-for-review-trade vs refund-threat as mirror-inverted (HIGH, documented). Applies contextual_scope correctly: scope of question matches loophole side, threat side as scope rival. Caveat well-structured (cuándo aplica B). Cites both expected atoms plus removal-broken-2025 as default. Notes loophole closure risk. Strong context awareness — explicitly distinguishes who initiates trade.

Q06.notes: Question explicitly time-anchored ('Estamos en 2026'). Detects pre-2025 vs broken-2025 conflict, applies temporal_supersession with full Antes/Desde/Hoy narrative referencing source IDs (wjAwMKuhxec, qv05SED0Z9E). Correctly preserves three-removal-reasons as historical context, not competing recommendation. Integrates loophole + call-playbook + dilution as scope-limited supplements.

Q07.notes: Detects price-for-max-occupancy vs extra-guest-fee-leverage (HIGH, documented). Applies contextual_scope with primary=extra-guest-fee-leverage as default 2026 (recency-justified). Caveat captures both scopes (new listing vs mature). Could have framed primary/secondary asymmetry slightly more cautiously since meta documents it as scope-split rather than winner — but overall sound. Cites both expected atoms.

Q08.notes: Multi-question expecting 3 conflicts (max-occupancy-vs-extra-fee + 5%-vs-10% + new-listing zone). Surfaced ONE undeclared conflict (calendar-strategy vs PL bootstrap) with well-formed proposed_contradictions entry. Did NOT surface the documented max-occupancy-vs-extra-fee or 5%-vs-10% conflicts despite citing the relevant atoms (more-reviews-beats-higher-rating, five-percent-adjustments via reciprocity flow). Misses required multi-conflict caveat structure.

Q09.notes: Undeclared zone, no false-positive conflict invented. Reframes the question (IB and Superhost are not competing variables) — strong context_awareness. Clean response with numbered steps, [[atom]] cites. No proposed_contradictions (correctly: nothing to propose). No forced temporal narrative.

Q10.notes: Undeclared zone — could have proposed a contradiction around min-stay 1 vs 2-3 nights since the response itself navigates between those values (base 2 + holiday spikes 3-5 + orphan 1). The response is internally coherent but misses opportunity to formalize tension as proposed_contradictions[]. No false detection. Clean format.

Q11.notes: Multi-question, prioritizes dilution (high-confidence) over removal (broken default + low-odds fallback) — exactly the expected_resolution. Detects review-removal conflict, applies temporal_supersession with full narrative. Cites all 4 expected atoms (more-dilutes-negative, removal-process-broken-2025, removal-call-playbook, damage-claim-for-removal-odds) plus loophole and refund-threat. Excellent operational sequencing (30-day plan).

Q12.notes: Undeclared zone, no false conflict. Strong gating logic (4 entry conditions). Honest about 'invisible Airbnb value' (dependencies). No spurious caveats. Clean response with relevant atoms in direct-booking topic. No forced temporal narrative.

Q13.notes: Undeclared zone, no false conflict invented. Prioritized response (base layer + filter-match fixes). All citations use full path 'notes/listing-optimization--...' — slightly verbose vs canonical [[atom-slug]] but functional. Closing 'Fuentes principales' line is somewhat informal. No proposed_contradictions (correctly: no genuine tension surfaced).

Q14.notes: Multi-question expecting 3 simultaneous pricing conflicts. Surfaced ONLY Thu/Sun (contextual_scope, well-handled). Did NOT surface 5%-vs-10% (uses 5% directly without caveat) and did NOT surface max-occupancy-vs-extra-fee (cites extra-guest-charge-tiers but not ranking--price-for-max-occupancy as rival). Cites 4 of 5 expected atoms (missing ranking--price-for-max-occupancy). Misses one-caveat-per-active-conflict requirement of §4.5.7.

Q15.notes: Undeclared zone, no false conflict. Strong reframing (search-rank-unknowable, conversion-over-views). Prioritized 8-step diagnosis with concrete benchmarks. Doesn't force a contradiction where none surfaces from the shortlist. Clean format, [[atom]] cites consistent.

global_observations: Strong O4v2 performance: temporal_supersession is consistently and correctly applied with structured Antes/Desde/Hoy narratives (Q01, Q06, Q11). Contextual_scope is well-handled with explicit primary=null when meta declares no universal winner (Q03). Single-conflict probes are near-flawless (Q01-Q07, Q09, Q11-Q13, Q15). The main weakness is multi-conflict questions (Q08, Q14): the agent surfaces only one of the 2-3 expected conflicts, missing the §4.5.7 'one caveat per active conflict' requirement. Q08 demonstrates good auto-curate behavior (well-formed proposed_contradictions for an undeclared tension). No false-positive conflicts invented in undeclared zones (Q09, Q10, Q12, Q13, Q15). Q10 missed an opportunity to propose a min-stay contradiction. Tone consistent and §10.7-compliant; no 'cabe destacar', actionable numbered steps, [[atom]] cites throughout.
